PBL Group Builder

JABSOM Problem-Based Learning group assignment — MD1–MD7

EXAMPLE data тАФ not a real roster. Edit freely to explore, then load your own file or start from the template.

Copy rows from Excel or Google Sheets (include the header row) and paste below. One sheet at a time тАФ the matching sheet is detected from the column headers.

View and edit one class year at a time.
How it works

A quick guide to building PBL groups. Re-open it anytime from ? in the header.

1 ┬╖ Load your roster

Open an .xlsx or .csv, Paste rows from Excel / Google Sheets, or start from the Template. Edit cells inline and add people with + Add. Switch between Students, Tutors, and Conflicts with the tabs.

2 ┬╖ Build the groups

On the Groups tab, choose the unit and class year, then Solve. The solver keeps every hard rule тАФ conflicts, no repeat tutor across units, schedule fit, and LC mentor тЙа tutor тАФ while balancing the soft preferences. Drag students between groups, lock the ones you want kept, and re-solve around them.

3 ┬╖ Export

Download the workbook. That file becomes your master тАФ open it next unit and the history carries forward so groups reshuffle. You can also Print / Save a PDF roster.

Good to know

  • Groups are built automatically from the tutors you assign to a unit тАФ one group per tutor. You don't define groups by hand.
  • Each tutor has a default meeting time; give a unit its own time only if it differs.
  • The Schedule tab is optional тАФ only for blocking recurring weekly times (a course, or a role like a HOME manager).
  • Everything runs in your browser, fully offline. No student data ever leaves your computer.
Loading example dataтАж New here? Start from the template (toolbar above), fill it in, then open it back here. You can also paste rows from Excel/Sheets.
Roster looks good?

Pick the unit to build and which class year's students to place, then Solve.

Advanced settings

Soft-preference weights тАФ higher means the solver tries harder to satisfy that preference. Defaults work for most cohorts.

Drag students to adjust; lock to keep on re-solve.
Pick a unit above and press Solve groups to build the groups.
Done? Save your work:

Runs entirely in your browser тАФ no data leaves your computer.

How it works тАФ 3 steps

  1. Load your roster тАФ Open an .xlsx/.csv, paste rows, or start from the Template. Edit on the Roster tab.
  2. Build groups тАФ go to the Groups tab, pick the unit (and class year), then Solve. Drag to adjust and lock students, then re-solve if needed.
  3. Export тАФ download the workbook. That file becomes your master тАФ open it next unit so history carries forward.

Groups are built automatically from the tutors you assign to a unit. The Schedule tab is optional.
